sources.name: Flesch-Kincaid Grade Level Formula
sources.note: FK 6-8 established as general-purpose plain language default

sources.name: Plain Language Act 2010
sources.note: Audience-first principle and default readability standard

sources.name: Strunk & White, Elements of Style
sources.note: Active voice and concision as default composition stance

_description: The fallback state for every variable in the system. If domain_register.json returns no match, if a word has no primary_vocab entry, if conflict_resolution.json finds no rule — this file defines what the LLM does next. Every rule file in the pipeline defers here on a miss.
_pipeline_step: fallback — consulted any time a rule or lookup returns no result

domain.reason: Safest assumption when domain cannot be detected from input

fk_target.reason: Plain Language Act standard for general public documents

register_ceiling.reason: Permits common concepts without requiring domain knowledge

voice.reason: Active voice as default per Strunk & White Rule 14 and Plain Language Act

sentence_length.reason: Primary FK driver. 15-20 word average produces FK 6-8 when paired with 1-2 syllable words

vocab_tier.reason: Always attempt primary_vocab.json first. Fallback only when primary cannot carry precise meaning

weight.reason: Soft weight allows conflict_resolution.json to permit exceptions. Hard rules never use this default

paragraph_length.reason: One idea per paragraph. New idea means new paragraph regardless of sentence count

adjectives_per_noun.reason: One adjective per noun by default. Two only if each adds distinct non-overlapping meaning

passive_voice_limit.value: permitted only when actor is unknown, irrelevant, or deliberately withheld
passive_voice_limit.reason: No two passive constructions in sequence

tiebreaker.reason: When two valid constructions exist, the shorter one wins

miss_behaviour.word_not_in_any_vocab: use shortest common English word that carries the meaning
